name: Atom

phase_0.boundary_condition: The ionization limit defines the absolute energy threshold beyond which the electron is no longer bound to the nucleus, closing the atomic system.

VINIT.justification: The initial void state of the atom before any electrons are captured or energy is applied, representing the unexcited genesis.

TANCH.justification: The terminal boundary that contains the entire atomic system, defining the absolute threshold beyond which the electron is no longer bound.

AFWD.justification: The forward morphism of an electron ascending to a higher discrete energy level through photon absorption.

AREV.justification: The reverse morphism of an electron descending to a lower energy level, releasing energy back to the environment.

CLINK.justification: The sequential chaining of multiple orbital jumps as an electron relaxes through intermediate energy states.

IMSCRIB.justification: The self-identical, stable configuration where the atom exists in its lowest energy form, recognizing its own baseline.

FSPLIT.justification: The co-multiplication that branches a single unsplit energy term into a fine structure doublet based on electron spin alignment.

FFUSE.justification: The multiplication that reconstitutes the fine structure doublet back into the original unsplit energy term in the LS coupling limit.

EVALT.justification: The affirmative evaluation where the electron remains trapped within the discrete potential well of the nucleus.

EVALF.justification: The negative evaluation where the electron overcomes the binding energy and escapes into the unbound continuum.

ENGAGR.justification: The paradice where the electron occupies multiple orbital states simultaneously, held without resolution until measurement.

IFIX.justification: The permanent, append-only record of a specific transition frequency, collapsing the superposition into an observable spectral line.

phase_3.void_description: The bare nucleus devoid of electrons, representing the uninitialized state before atomic formation.
phase_3.true_description: The bound state where the electron is successfully captured and maintained within a discrete orbital.
phase_3.false_description: The ionized state where the electron has escaped the potential well into the continuum.
phase_3.both_description: The quantum superposition where the electron exists in a linear combination of bound and unbound states simultaneously.

steps.domain_action: bare nucleus initializes the atomic system in the void state before electron capture
cascade transition chains sequential orbital jumps during electron relaxation
ground state establishes the self-identical baseline configuration of the atom
spin-orbit splitting branches the unsplit energy term into a fine structure doublet
bound excitation drives the forward morphism of the electron within the T-arm
bound state affirms the electron remains trapped in the discrete potential well
photon emission drives the reverse morphism of the electron within the F-arm
continuum state evaluates the negative branch where the electron escapes
spin-orbit recombination reconstitutes the doublet back into the unsplit energy term
quantum superposition enters the paradice of simultaneous orbital occupation
spectral measurement permanently records the transition frequency as an observable line
ionization limit closes the system boundary at the absolute threshold of electron binding

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Schrödinger equation translates physical potentials into quantized wavefunctions
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: electromagnetic field mediates the exchange of photons between the atom and the environment
phase_5.memory_mechanism: electron configuration stores the current quantum state and orbital occupations
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Hamiltonian operator determines the time evolution and energy transitions of the system
phase_5.alfs_store: NIST Atomic Spectra Database serves as the canonical reference store for all atomic energy levels and transition rates

phase_6.cycle_cost: photon energy exchange represents the quantized energy absorbed or emitted during state transitions
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: initial quantum state with specific orbital occupation and phase coherence
phase_6.post_cycle_state: final quantum state after transition or measurement with updated energy and entropy
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 because the unitary evolution of the isolated atom conserves von Neumann entropy with information preserved in the entangled atom-photon system

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → CLINK → IMSCRIB → FSPLIT → AFWD → EVALT → AREV → EVALF → FFUSE → ENGAGR → IFIX → TANCH
-- Class: Atom
-- Fingerprint: sig=(6,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=12
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(3, 8)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑱  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑼 → 𐑠  | composition — regime coherence
--   [2] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑱 → 𐑚  | identity — self-imscription
--   [3] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [4] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [5] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [6] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [7] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [8] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑳  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [9] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑙 → 𐑭  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [10] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑳 → 𐑡  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [11] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑭 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def atom_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑱)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (bare nucleus initializes the atomic system in the void state before electron ...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑼 𐑠)  -- [1] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (cascade transition chains sequential orbital jumps during electron relaxation)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑱 𐑚)  -- [2] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (ground state establishes the self-identical baseline configuration of the atom)
  -- FSPLIT [3] (gran := 𐑚) (spin-orbit splitting branches the unsplit energy term into a fine structure doublet) / FFUSE [8] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (3 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [4] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (bound excitation drives the forward morphism of the electron within the T-arm)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (bound state affirms the electron remains trapped in the discrete potential well)
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (photon emission drives the reverse morphism of the electron within the F-arm)
      -- F-branch (1 nodes)
      (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (continuum state evaluates the negative branch where the electron escapes))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [8]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑳)  -- [8] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (spin-orbit recombination reconstitutes the doublet back into the unsplit energy term)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑙 𐑭)  -- [9] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (quantum superposition enters the paradice of simultaneous orbital occupation)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑳 𐑡)  -- [10] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (spectral measurement permanently records the transition frequency as an obser...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑭 𐑼)  -- [11] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (ionization limit closes the system boundary at the absolute threshold of elec...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def atom_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (atom_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def atom_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (atom_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem atom_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
